name: code-simplification
description: Reduces accidental complexity while preserving behavior

positive_triggers.input: Refactor this 500-line module, it's hard to understand
positive_triggers.reason: Accidental complexity, needs Chesterton's Fence + behavior preservation

positive_triggers.input: I need to add a feature but touching 5 files for one change seems wrong
Can you clean up this function? It works but has too many nested ifs
positive_triggers.reason: Working code that's harder to read than it should be

negative_triggers.input: Remove the AI-generated comments and emoji from this code
negative_triggers.reason: AI slop cleanup, use omo's remove-ai-slops skill

negative_triggers.input: Rewrite this entire module from scratch

behavioral_evals.scenario: User asks: 'Refactor this 500-line module, hard to understand'
expected_behavior: Agent identifies specific target function/class (not 'refactor everything')
Agent runs git blame / history on lines to be removed (Chesterton's Fence)
Agent runs test suite to establish baseline (must pass before)
Agent applies techniques in order: rename → extract → inline → remove dead → flatten → co-locate
Agent verifies behavior unchanged via same test suite (still pass)
Net diff is negative or near-zero (removed ≥ added)

behavioral_evals.scenario: User asks: 'Add a feature but touching 5 files seems wrong'
expected_behavior: Agent investigates the 5-file spread to find accidental coupling
Agent proposes simplification that reduces coupling (not just adds the feature)
Agent ensures tests still pass before AND after simplification
Agent does NOT simplify unrelated code in the same diff

notes: Behavioral evals are warning-level until promoted via upstream issue tracker.
